---
pagetitle: "MayaVoice — AI Translation for Mayan Languages"
title: ""
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-section}

# 🌽 MayaVoice

::: {.subtitle}
Breaking the language barrier for 6 million Mayan speakers in Guatemala through AI-powered translation.
:::

[Try the Demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/DanielRegaladoCardoso/mayavoice){.cta-button}
[View Results](results.qmd){.cta-button .secondary}

::: {.hero-stats}
::: {.stat}
[14]{.stat-number}
[Languages]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[6M+]{.stat-number}
[Speakers]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[40.28]{.stat-number}
[BLEU Score]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[35K+]{.stat-number}
[Dictionary Entries]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

:::

## The Problem {.section-header}

::: {.section-subtitle}
Millions of Maya speakers in Guatemala face critical barriers to healthcare, education, justice, and government services — simply because these services operate exclusively in Spanish.
:::

::: {.problem-grid}

::: {.problem-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🏥
:::
### Healthcare
Patients cannot describe symptoms or understand diagnoses in Spanish. Medical miscommunication leads to misdiagnosis, treatment errors, and preventable deaths in rural Maya communities.
:::

::: {.problem-card}
::: {.card-icon}
⚖️
:::
### Justice
Maya speakers face legal proceedings conducted entirely in Spanish. Without adequate translation, defendants cannot understand charges, testify effectively, or access their legal rights.
:::

::: {.problem-card}
::: {.card-icon}
📚
:::
### Education
Children who speak K'iche', Q'eqchi', or Mam at home are taught exclusively in Spanish. This linguistic gap is a primary driver of dropout rates in indigenous communities.
:::

::: {.problem-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🏛️
:::
### Government
Despite constitutional recognition of 14 Mayan languages, government services, forms, and official communications remain almost exclusively in Spanish.
:::

:::

## The Opportunity

Guatemala legally recognizes 14 Mayan languages, and its constitution mandates access to public services in indigenous languages. Yet no scalable translation technology existed — until now.

::: {.market-grid}

::: {.market-item}
#### Government
Municipalities serving 6M+ Maya speakers need translation for public services, legal proceedings, and official communications.
:::

::: {.market-item}
#### Healthcare
Hospitals and clinics in the highlands require translation for patient intake, diagnosis, and treatment instructions.
:::

::: {.market-item}
#### NGOs & Development
International organizations working in Guatemala need translation for community outreach, surveys, and program delivery.
:::

::: {.market-item}
#### Education
Bilingual education programs require translation tools for curriculum development and teacher support.
:::

:::

## Our Solution

MayaVoice is the **first AI-powered translation system** built specifically for Guatemalan Mayan languages. It covers 14 languages across 4 linguistic branches, serving the vast majority of Maya speakers in the country.

Unlike generic translation services that ignore low-resource languages, MayaVoice was trained on authentic Mayan language data — parallel texts, curated dictionaries, and verified linguistic resources — combined with a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that grounds translations in verified vocabulary.

::: {.metric-highlight}
[40.28 BLEU · 55.07 chrF]{.metric-value}

Evaluated on 300 held-out test sentences across all 14 languages. Top-performing languages (Mam, Kaqchikel) achieve BLEU scores above 40 — competitive with commercial translation systems for much higher-resource language pairs.
:::

::: {.maya-callout}
> MayaVoice doesn't just translate words — it bridges a centuries-old communication gap between Guatemala's indigenous majority and the institutions that serve them.
:::

## What's Next

We are actively expanding MayaVoice's capabilities:

- **Conversational AI assistant** for real-time bilingual support
- **Voice input/output** for speakers who may not read/write in their language
- **Domain-specific models** for healthcare, legal, and education contexts
- **Community validation** with native speakers for continuous quality improvement
- **Mobile-first deployment** for use in rural areas with limited connectivity

---

::: {.section-header}
## Get Involved

MayaVoice is an open research project. We welcome collaborations with linguists, native speakers, NGOs, government agencies, and anyone working to preserve and promote Mayan languages.
:::

[Try the Demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/DanielRegaladoCardoso/mayavoice){.cta-button}
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/DanielRegaladoUMiami/mayavoice-llm){.cta-button .secondary}
